nativeRange.habitat: Coastal reef slopes over rubble and sand at depths of roughly 3–50 m; tends to associate with alpheid shrimp burrows

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding primarily on zooplankton and small crustaceans suspended in the water column. In captivity generally accepts mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, krill, and frozen carnivore preparations. Has been known to pick food off the substrate as well. Multiple small feedings per day are generally advised, especially during acclimation.

summary: The Blue Gudgeon Dartfish (Lyretail) is a peaceful, reef-safe dartfish from the Indo-Pacific, generally considered beginner-friendly. It tends to hover near the sand bed and is known to be a jumper, so a tightly sealed lid is generally advised.
careNotes:
Generally advised to keep in an established reef or fish-only tank of at least 30 gallons (about 114 L) with a sandy substrate and coral rubble for shelter. This species has been known to inhabit burrows excavated by alpheid pistol shrimp and will co-habit peacefully with shrimp of the Alpheus genus. It tends to be shy during acclimation and may hide for several days when first introduced; multiple small feedings are generally advisable to encourage feeding responses early on.

This species is widely regarded as a jumper — a tight-fitting lid with no gaps is generally considered essential. Like most dartfish, the Blue Gudgeon Dartfish is considered a high escape risk, and even small gaps around equipment are enough for it to slip through.

Best kept singly or as a mated pair; the species has been known to be tolerant of other dartfish species, though bonded pairs tend to stay closely together. Generally considered peaceful toward all tank mates.

Most hobbyist sources agree on a salinity of SG 1.020–1.025, though some reef-oriented references suggest keeping closer to natural seawater at SG 1.026.
breedingNotes: Breeding in captivity has not been well documented in hobbyist literature. In the wild, this species has been reported to live in pairs sharing a burrow. Captive spawning attempts are rare and specific breeding protocols are not established in commonly available sources.

additional.notes: Cross-checked sizing (5 in / 12.7 cm), tank size (30 gal), care level, temperament, diet, and reef safety. Confirmed peaceful, reef-safe, carnivore, 30 gal minimum.

additional.notes: Cross-checked size (12 cm), temperature (~26°C), salinity (1.026), minimum tank (100 L), and grouping behavior. Confirmed always reef safe, very shy/docile, pairs recommended.

additional.name: AquariumDomain
additional.notes: Note: page covers Ptereleotris heteroptera (a closely related species also called Blue Gudgeon Dartfish). Confirmed 5 in size, 30 gal, easy care, peaceful, reef safe, carnivore, temp 72–80°F. Used for parameter averaging only.

fish.breedingNotes: Captive breeding protocols are not established in commonly available hobbyist sources. In the wild, pairs share burrows and are presumably substrate-spawners, but specific aquarium breeding has not been well documented.
